title: US Checks Other Countries for Unfair Work Practices
summary: The United States is starting a new investigation to look at 60 countries, including China, the European Union, Canada, and the UK. They want to see if these countries are allowing goods made with forced labor to be sold, which might hurt American businesses. Forced labor means people are made to work against their will, often in bad conditions. The US says this gives foreign companies an unfair advantage because they can make things cheaper. This investigation could lead to new taxes called tariffs on goods from these countries. The US has done similar checks before, like with China, and has laws to stop goods from places where forced labor happens. Public hearings will happen in April to talk about this issue.

Why does the US think forced labor hurts American businesses?
options: It gives foreign companies an unfair advantage
questions.correct_answer: It gives foreign companies an unfair advantage

questions.question: What could happen if the investigation finds problems?
Which country was mentioned as having a previous forced labor law?

background_read: Forced labor is a serious issue where people are forced to work without pay or choice, often in unsafe places. Countries sometimes make rules to stop goods made this way from being sold. The US is checking if other countries are following these rules to protect American companies from unfair competition. This is part of trade, which is how countries buy and sell goods with each other.
Article_Structure: WHO: The US government and its Trade Representative, Jamieson Greer, are investigating 60 countries including China, the EU, Canada, and the UK.
WHAT: They are checking if these countries allow goods made with forced labor to be sold, which might hurt US businesses.
WHERE: This involves countries around the world like Australia, Brazil, and Japan.
WHY: To stop unfair competition and protect American workers and companies from cheaper goods made with forced labor.

perspectives.description: Believes forced labor gives foreign companies an unfair advantage and wants to enforce rules to protect American businesses.
